hypothesis: If diffusion-model generated crystal structures trained on Materials Project and OQMD data, when relaxed via DFT, exhibit lower energy above hull, smaller RMSD, higher validity (min interatomic distances > 0.5 Å), and closer alignment to real-material distributions across SUN, EMD, Fréchet distance, and CrystalNN precision/recall than structures produced by perturbing existing materials, then diffusion models provide a more effective framework for stable and realistic materials discovery.
